news.summary: The US Senate recently voted 66-32 in favor of advancing the GENIUS Act, aimed at regulating stablecoins, with 16 Democrats supporting it despite concerns over politicians' ties to cryptocurrency firms. Following the vote, Colorado Senator Michael Bennet introduced the STABLE GENIUS Act to prevent members of Congress from issuing or investing in digital currency. The article lists 11 US senators who have investments in crypto firms, including Tim Sheehy (Montana) with $1,001-$15,000 in Intercontinental Exchange, Steve Daines (Republican) who sold shares in crypto-related ETFs, Jackey Rosen (Nevada) holding an investment in PayPal's stablecoin, Dan Sullivan (Alaska) owning shares in BlackRock ETFs, Markwayne Mullin (Oklahoma) having investments in Intercontinental Exchange and BlackRock, Tommy Tuberville (Alabama) with investments in PayPal, Katie Britt (Alabama) whose husband owns stock in Block, Bernie Moreno (Ohio) with shares in eToro, Shelley Capito (West Virginia) with investments in BlackRock, Dave McCormick (Pennsylvania) purchasing the Bitwise Bitcoin ETF, and Sheldon Whitehouse (Rhode Island) with investments in Tesla, Block, and PayPal. Lawmakers are required to disclose investments but efforts to prevent them from investing in regulated companies have faced challenges. The trend of lawmakers investing in crypto may continue as the sector grows.
news.headline: 11 US senators invest in crypto firms, vote leads to GENIUS Act passage. Legislators face backlash over ties to crypto.

Publicly listed company Genius Group resumes buying Bitcoin

news.summary: In Deerfield Beach, Florida, on May 22, 2025, troubling issues surrounding the well-being of young individuals are being highlighted. The prevalence of problems such as bullying, hopelessness, suicide, and mental health concerns among youth is alarming, with statistics showing that a significant portion of students - one out of every five - are affected. These challenges underscore the urgent need for effective interventions and support systems to safeguard the mental and emotional health of our youth.
news.headline: National charity Be Strong strives to improve mental health with Never Alone campaign, driven by students and corporate partners. Alarming stats show one in five students face mental health challenges.

news.summary: In Tampa, Florida on May 21, 2025, the relatives of a 16-year-old female victim who was reportedly killed by a 35-year-old male through the Grindr app have initiated legal action against Grindr, LLC. The lawsuit claims that the company's age verification process was insufficient, resulting in the tragic incident.
news.headline: Family of murdered 16-year-old sues Grindr for $750 million, alleging platform design enabled child predation.

news.summary: In the latest financial report, revenues for the initial quarter surged by 8.1% compared to the previous year, hitting a record high of $754.7 million. Net income for the same period also saw a substantial rise of 12.6% year over year, reaching a historic high of $19.3 million. These figures showcase impressive growth in the company's performance for the first quarter, indicating a strong start to the fiscal year.
news.headline: Formula Systems' first quarter revenues hit a record high of $754.7 million, up by 8.1%. Net income also rose by 12.6% to $19.3 million.

news.summary: Ross Stores, Inc. is set to announce its first-quarter earnings after market close on Thursday, May 22. Analysts predict the Dublin-based company will report earnings of $1.43 per share, a slight decrease from the $1.46 per share reported last year. Revenue is expected to reach $4.96 billion for the quarter, up from $4.86 billion the previous year. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of 40.5 cents per share. Ross Stores stock closed at $152.68, down 1% on Wednesday. For more details and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page.
news.headline: Ross Stores likely to report Q1 earnings of $1.43 per share (down from $1.46) on revenue of $4.96 billion ($4.86 billion YoY).

news.summary: Retail giant Target Corporation (NYSE: TGT) reported disappointing first-quarter results with adjusted earnings per share of $1.30, falling short of analysts' forecast of $1.64. The company's quarterly sales totaled $23.85 billion, marking a 2.8% decrease compared to the previous year and missing the expected $24.32 billion. Target's Chair and CEO, Brian Cornell, acknowledged the challenging market conditions and highlighted the company's focus on providing excellent assortment, experience, and value to customers. Despite the lower sales, there were positive aspects such as a significant 36% increase in same-day delivery through Target's digital platform.
news.headline: Target (NYSE: TGT) reports adjusted EPS of $1.30, missing estimates of $1.64. Q1 sales of $23.85 billion, down 2.8% YoY. CEO optimistic about digital growth.

news.headline: Allied Properties Real Estate Investment Trust sets net-zero emissions targets with Science Based Targets initiative approval for 1.5°C pathway.

news.summary: A recent real-world study revealed a noticeable decrease in severe bleeding and transfusion requirements among coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) patients taking the blood thinner ticagrelor when paired with CytoSorbents therapy. The analysis, conducted on May 22, 2025, in Princeton, New Jersey, showcased statistically significant results. The corporation at the helm of this advancement, CytoSorbents Corporation, listed on NASDAQ as CTSO, has been a pioneer in this field. This finding demonstrates a positive impact on patient outcomes, marking a promising development in the medical sphere.
news.headline: CytoSorbents therapy reduces bleeding in CABG patients on Ticagrelor; Significant decrease shown in EuroPCR 2025.

news.summary: New York, May 22, 2025 - Arena Investors, LP, a leading global institutional asset manager, proudly disclosed that the third vintage of their multi-strategy fund and related vehicles have massively surpassed the fundraising goal. The fund managed to draw in investments from a diverse group of global institutional investors, showcasing the growing interest in their offerings. This success indicates the confidence and trust that investors have in Arena's strategies, with the fund attracting substantial capital from around the world. The achievement underscores Arena's position as a preferred choice for investors seeking strong returns and strategic investment opportunities.
news.headline: Arena announced the closure of its third flagship Special Opportunities Fund, which exceeded its fundraising target, attracting capital from global institutional investors.

news.summary: A previous high-ranking executive from the CIA and an expert from Apple have been enlisted to assist in the advancement of Strider's unique AI-driven intelligence platform, Spark. The company, known for delivering top-tier strategic intelligence solutions, revealed this update on May 22, 2025, in Salt Lake City.
news.headline: Former CIA executive Anthony DeMario joins Strider as EVP to develop AI-powered intelligence engine Spark. Salt Lake City, May 22, 2025.

news.summary: In a recent announcement on May 22, 2025, Cyient Limited, a global company specializing in Intelligent Engineering solutions, appointed Kavita Kurup as their new Chief People Officer (CPO). Kavita will be leading the Human Resources responsibilities from the company's base in Hyderabad, India. This strategic appointment is aimed at enhancing the workforce management at Cyient Limited and signifies a key development in their organizational structure.
news.headline: Kavita Kurup appointed Chief People Officer of Cyient, a global engineering solutions company. Hyderabad-based.

These commitments comprise multi-unit contracts and the brand's forthcoming introduction into the Pennsylvania market.
news.headline: Eggs Up Grill expands with 5 new locations, 3 deals signed, approaching Pennsylvania market debut. #Growth #Breakfast

news.summary: Conventional techniques remain prevalent in finance, yet there is a gradual uptake of emerging technologies like artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, and advanced analytics. As of May 22, 2025, in New York and Montvale, N.J., there has been a rise in interest in these innovative tools. While traditional methods still dominate the sector, AI and advanced analytics are showing promising early results, with slow but increasing adoption rates. This shift signifies a changing landscape in finance and highlights the potential benefits of integrating these cutting-edge technologies.
news.headline: Deloitte, IMA survey shows AI gaining traction in cost acc & profit reporting, despite slow adoption. (85 chars)

news.summary: Unisys, a technology company based in Blue Bell, Pennsylvania, has recently been acknowledged for its top-notch technology and innovative solutions. The company has been recognized for its Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) framework and quantum risk assessments. Unisys was designated as a leader in the 2025 Attack Surface Management (ASM) report by a global analyst. This recognition highlights the company's expertise in managing security threats and staying ahead in the constantly evolving tech landscape. As a publicly traded company listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol UIS, Unisys continues to demonstrate its commitment to excellence in the technology sector.
news.headline: Unisys (NYSE: UIS) named leader in 2025 Attack Surface Management report for technology and risk assessment.

news.summary: Collaborative articles shed light on the practical, technological, and structural impacts for exchanges, clearing houses, and CSDs following the transition to T+1, marking one year since implementation. The series delves into the foreseeable future as international markets contemplate analogous changes. This initiative originates from Stockholm and was released on May 22, 2025.
news.headline: Vermiculus partners with GreySpark Partners for T+1 Settlement insights, explores implications for exchanges globally.

news.summary: A partnership has been announced on May 22, 2025, in Bridgewater, N.J., where Firstsource Solutions Limited, a prominent global provider of solutions and services (NSE:FSL) (BSE:532809) and a part of RP-Sanjiv Goenka Group, has joined forces with Sanas. Sanas is renowned for its real-time speech technology.

news.summary: The summer season sees a notable 20% rise in teenage traffic fatalities, with an average of 7 deaths daily, according to reports. Commencing with Memorial Day weekend, this period dubbed "the 100 deadliest days" by safety specialists marks a significant spike in accidents among teen drivers.
news.headline: National Road Safety Foundation urges young drivers & parents to prioritize safety during summer; 20% increase in teen traffic deaths, averaging 7 daily.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) revealed several recalls in partnership with certain companies. The recalls involve Nezmdi Infant Swings due to a suffocation hazard, posing a significant risk of serious injury or even death. Consumers are urged to visit www.cpsc.gov for more information on the recalls.
news.headline: CPSC recalls Nezmdi Infant Swings due to suffocation risk. Visit www.cpsc.gov for more info. Stay safe!

news.summary: A design firm recently released a report on their AIA 2030 Commitment, showcasing anticipated energy consumption data for over 4 million square feet of projects. This data highlights the possibility of achieving excellent performance on a significant scale. The report was made public on May 22, 2025, from LPA Design in Irvine, California.
news.headline: LPA reduced energy use by 76.2% in 2024 across 4 million sq ft projects, showing high performance on large scale.

news.summary: On May 20, 2025, Pictor, a prominent provider of multiplex proteomics solutions, declared the opening of its U.S. base in Carlsbad, California. This move signifies a major step in their expansion plans and dedication to furthering advancements in the field.
news.headline: Pictor, a global leader in proteomics, opens U.S. HQ in California; boosting operations amid regulatory changes.

news.summary: Lake Victoria Gold Ltd., represented by USA News Group, released a news commentary on May 22, 2025, in Vancouver, BC. The commentary discusses the recent impressive performance of major gold producers. Concerning institutional coverage and market focus on the record-setting quarters of large gold producers, attention is drawn towards a potentially lucrative opportunity in the gold sector that may be less prominent but equally promising. This alternative perspective suggests a different outlook on the gold market, hinting at substantial potential growth.
news.headline: Gold producers hitting record-setting quarters. Contrarian Capital eyeing next wave of gold market growth.

news.summary: TotalEnergies SE announced the opening of its largest solar power complex in Europe near Seville, Spain. The cluster consists of five solar farms with a total capacity of 263 megawatts. These facilities are projected to produce 515 gigawatt-hours of renewable energy per year, sufficient to power over 150,000 homes in Spain and reduce carbon emissions by approximately 245,000 tons annually. The company plans to sell most of the electricity generated through long-term power purchase agreements (PPAs), while the rest will be available on the wholesale energy market.
news.headline: TotalEnergies opens Europe's largest solar complex in Spain, with 263MW capacity to power 150,000 homes and reduce carbon emissions by 245,000 tons/yr.

news.summary: On Thursday, Rio Tinto plc (NYSE: RIO) announced that CEO Jakob Stausholm will be stepping down from his position later this year. Stausholm, who initially joined the company in 2018 as executive director and CFO, assumed the CEO role in January 2021. Rio Tinto Chair Dominic Barton commended Stausholm for his leadership in rebuilding trust with key stakeholders. Barton emphasized that the company's strategic priorities remain constant, with a focus on enhancing operational performance to maximize asset potential. Additionally, Rio Tinto revealed plans to commence preliminary activities for expanding production at the Amrun bauxite mine in Far North Queensland. Bitcoin also hit a milestone by surpassing $111,000.
news.headline: Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm stepping down as company launches $1.9B bauxite expansion plan; aims for greater operational performance.

news.summary: Clothing company V.F. Corporation (NYSE: VFC) released its fourth-quarter results, revealing an adjusted earnings per share of a 13-cent loss, surpassing the anticipated 14-cent loss. Despite this positive outcome, the quarterly sales stood at $2.14 billion, showing a 5% decline compared to the previous year and falling short of the expected $2.18 billion. President and CEO Bracken Darrell expressed satisfaction with the progress of VF's transformation process, highlighting the successful achievement of cost reduction and balance sheet strengthening goals by FY25. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: VF Corp reported Q4 EPS of 13 cents loss (beating street view of 14 cents loss) and $2.14B in sales (down 5% YoY). CEO highlights progress in transforming the company.
